name: Liège

overview.description: Liège is a French-speaking industrial city on the Meuse river, known for its striking modern railway station, a historic hilltop staircase, and a lively market and café culture along the riverbanks.
overview.foundedNote: A major industrial and university centre in French-speaking Wallonia
whyVisit: Liège-Guillemins railway station — a landmark steel-and-glass structure designed by Santiago Calatrava
Montagne de Bueren — a steep 374-step staircase (built 1875-1880) with panoramic views from the top
La Batte — one of Belgium's oldest and largest Sunday markets, running along the Meuse river
Grand Curtius museum for art, archaeology and decorative arts

attractions.name: Liège-Guillemins Station
attractions.description: A striking modern railway station designed by Santiago Calatrava, notable for its steel-and-glass canopy.

attractions.name: Montagne de Bueren
attractions.description: A 374-step staircase built 1875-1880, honouring the defenders of Liège against Charles the Bold; rewards climbers with panoramic city views.

attractions.name: Grand Curtius Museum
attractions.description: A museum of art, archaeology and decorative arts housed in a historic mansion overlooking the Meuse.

attractions.name: La Batte Market
attractions.description: One of Belgium's oldest and largest markets, held every Sunday along the Meuse riverbank.

transportation.description: TEC operates buses and a modern tram line through the city centre.
Liège-Guillemins is a major rail hub with direct connections to Brussels, Namur, Maastricht and Germany.

hotels.name: City Centre (near Place du Marché)
hotels.notes: Central location near the historic market square and riverside cafés.

foodSpots.name: Restaurants near the city centre
foodSpots.notes: Fewer dedicated Indian restaurants than Brussels or Antwerp; the university population supports a broader international food scene.

tips: French is the primary language in Liège — Dutch is much less commonly spoken here than in Flanders.
The Night of the Hills (Nocturne des Coteaux), on the first Saturday of October, lines Montagne de Bueren with candles.
La Batte Sunday market is a good spot for browsing local produce and goods along the river.
